mutation_test.procedure: Manually swapped Unlock's two statements in engine/btree/latch.go (s.releaseLatch(nodeID, l) moved before l.mu.Unlock(), unlockOrderHook call kept textually between them), ran the new test, then restored the original (correct) ordering. The swap was never committed -- verified via `git status`/`git diff` showing latch.go clean of this swap at commit time.
mutation_test.reversed_ordering_result: go test ./engine/btree/... -race -run TestNodeLatchUnlockOrderingPreventsDoubleLock -v -count=3 -> FAIL x3, each with the explicit diagnostic: 'double-lock detected for node 1: the original latch (l1) is still locked while a second, different latch object (l2) was concurrently locked for the same node ID...'
mutation_test.restored_ordering_result: same command after restoring correct order -> PASS x5
mutation_test.conclusion: the new test has real teeth: it deterministically fails under the reversed ordering and deterministically passes under the correct ordering, on every run (no flakiness observed in either direction).

full_suite_self_consistency.result: PASS -- all tests including the 6 pre-existing TestNodeLatch* tests and the new TestNodeLatchUnlockOrderingPreventsDoubleLock, plus the rest of the package (insert/delete/lookup/serialization/prefix-scan tests). Total run time ~355s (dominated by pre-existing TestCrabbingInsert/VeryDeepOverlappingSubtree, unrelated to this change).

scope_check: git status --short engine/btree/ shows only latch.go and latch_test.go modified; no files outside engine/btree/ touched by this run's implementation changes
